candidate_problems.text: What concrete architectural mechanisms would let an LLM-based system generate candidate explanatory structures and subject them to criticism in a way that is not reducible to next-token continuation?
What operational criterion distinguishes 'participating in knowledge creation' from merely supplying conjectural variety within a larger epistemic system?
Can a system optimized for plausible continuation be repurposed for explanatory search without inheriting failure modes that systematically block criticism-sensitive improvement?
What kinds of criticism and selection pressures are sufficient to transform raw linguistic conjectures into hard-to-vary explanations?
Is the claim that knowledge creation is substrate-independent compatible with cases where representational substrate constrains what criticisms can even be formulated?
